data.title: Hold Rating on First Capital REIT Reflects Fair but Limited Upside in Takeover Offer
data.description:
First Capital Realty, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Mark Rothschild from Canaccord Genuity downgraded the rating on the stock to a Hold and gave it a C$24.40 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mark Rothschild has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, chiefly that First Capital REIT has agreed to be acquired at a valuation he views as fair but not exceptional for such a high‑quality, hard‑to‑replicate portfolio. The offer implies a modest premium to his net asset value estimate and recent trading levels, and although it locks in solid gains for investors over the past year, it does not provide enough additional upside to justify a continued Buy stance.

He also notes that the consideration mix of cash and Choice Properties units leaves the final value somewhat dependent on Choice’s share price and that the deal is expected to close only in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory review. Given these terms, the transaction largely caps further appreciation in First Capital’s units, so he raises his target price to the offer level and moves to a Hold recommendation, reflecting limited remaining return potential relative to risk.

Rothschild covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as Killam Apartment REIT Un, RioCan Real Estate Investment, and Firm Capital Property. According to TipRanks, Rothschild has an average return of 10.4% and a 69.09% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, CIBC also downgraded the stock to a Hold with a C$24.40 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating on Choice Properties: Strategic First Capital REIT Acquisition Enhances Urban Retail Portfolio and Long-Term Growth Outlook
data.description:
Mark Rothschild, an analyst from Canaccord Genuity, maintained the Buy rating on Choice Properties Real Estate Investment. The associated price target remains the same with C$17.50.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mark Rothschild has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Choice Properties’ strategic acquisition of First Capital REIT and its valuation. He views the FCR portfolio as a strong fit with Choice’s existing necessity-based retail assets in major Canadian cities, expecting the deal—despite near‑term dilution—to enhance long-term NOI, FFO growth, and valuation multiples.

He also notes that Choice’s units trade at an implied cap rate below peers and at a modest premium to NAV, supporting further upside to his target price. The transaction is expected to upgrade portfolio quality by increasing exposure to dense urban markets, diversifying tenant concentration away from Loblaw while maintaining strong necessity-based tenancy, and leveraging Choice’s scale and balance sheet, which together justify maintaining a Buy recommendation and $17.50 target.

Rothschild covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as Killam Apartment REIT Un, RioCan Real Estate Investment, and Firm Capital Property. According to TipRanks, Rothschild has an average return of 10.4% and a 69.09% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hold Rating on Northwest Natural: Improving Growth and Diversification Already Priced Into Shares
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Shelby Tucker maintained a Hold rating on Northwest Gas today and set a price target of $58.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shelby Tucker has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that balance Northwest Natural’s improving growth profile with a valuation that already reflects much of this upside. The company is shifting from a slow-growing regional utility toward a more diversified platform, adding high-growth Texas gas assets and water utilities that should lift long-term EPS growth, especially if the MX3 storage expansion proceeds as planned.

At the same time, Tucker notes that the shares have recently outperformed, and his sum-of-the-parts analysis suggests the current price already embeds a premium for constructive regulation and Texas-driven opportunities. While multi-year rate cases in Oregon and Washington and rising Texas capex support more predictable earnings, first-mover regulatory risk and a still gas-heavy earnings mix temper the upside, leading him to recommend holding rather than buying at present levels.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
